view.bannerSub: There is more than one way to connect. Even for the same data, <0>which value you connect on</0> makes a different connection. You can keep each as <1>its own connection</1>, and questions work across all of them automatically.

empty.title: No connections yet
empty.sub: Look for values that appear in two or more of your datasets, and build a connection from them.
empty.btn: Look for pairs that could connect

view.perspectiveHint: {{count}} · each a different way to connect

summary.note: "Values found in both" is how many "{{concept}}" values matched across datasets (separate from the total number of facts). "What they connect on" is how many things this connection matches by.

view.toolsHint: How many datasets report this value? and the like, answered on the spot (no AI involved).

relation.subPropertyOf: is a part of the field

align.head: Match up two connections
align.hint: When words from two separately built connections mean the same thing, you can record that match (it becomes a citable fact, and you can undo it later).
align.noSrcTerms: This connection has no words that can be matched up.
align.noTgtTerms: Nothing of the same sort (kind / field) on the other side. Pick a different connection on the right.
align.pickDistinct: Pick a different word on the left and on the right.
align.needTwo: Once you have two or more connections, you can match them up here (you have {{count}}).
align.sameTerm: These two already use the same word — nothing to match up.
align.groundingCount: {{n}} of them match a word to an external standard. You can also review or undo those on each dataset's page.
align.strayCount: {{n}} of them involve words that are not part of your current connections.

builder.subtitle: Pick <0>two or more existing datasets</0>, declare the <1>concept to connect</1> (composition, crystal system, author…) and the column that represents it, and you can build a <2>bridge that connects across datasets (a crosswalk)</2> on the shared value. It is an ontology built <3>from data you already have</3>, not from a new CSV.

tooFew.title: Fewer than two shared datasets to connect
tooFew.sub: Once two or more datasets have been "published for search", you can build a cross-dataset crosswalk here.

builder.step1: 1. Pick the datasets to connect (two or more)
builder.step2Hint: e.g. composition / crystal_system / author (an alphanumeric key)
builder.conceptVocab: Words auto-minted from "{{key}}" → kind <0>xw:{{className}}</0> · linking field <1>xw:has{{className}}</1>
builder.conceptInvalid: Enter an alphanumeric key (e.g. crystal_system). Put a Japanese display name into "this perspective's name".
builder.step3: 3. For each dataset, pick "the field that carries {{key}}"
builder.apiKeyPlaceholder: Anthropic API key (used for AI suggestions; stored only on this device)
builder.proposeNote: The AI suggested {{count}} fields (please review and adjust).
builder.proposeNone: The AI could not find a field carrying "{{key}}". Please pick one manually.
builder.step4: 4. Criterion for treating values as the same (normalization)
builder.step4Hint: How far to treat surface variants as identical

norm.whitespace: Ignore whitespace differences (collapse leading/trailing and runs)
norm.loose_text: Loose match (ignore case, whitespace and width together)
norm.element_canonical: Align composition + element order too (Bi2Te3 = Te3Bi2)

normHint.identity: Joins only values that match exactly (the default usable for any concept).
normHint.casefold: Ignores only upper/lower case differences (e.g. FeO = feo). Not for compositions (Co ≠ CO).
normHint.whitespace: Ignores only leading/trailing and repeated whitespace differences.
normHint.nfkc: Normalizes full-width and compatibility characters (e.g. half-width kana) before matching.
normHint.loose_text: Loose match ignoring case, whitespace and width together (does not reorder).
normHint.composition: Absorbs only subscript/whitespace differences (element order is distinguished).
normHint.element_canonical: Joins as the same composition even if element order differs (formula = multiset).
normHint.recipe: Arrange the steps (primitives) yourself to build your own "treat as the same value" criterion.

builder.extraCond: Extra match conditions (all must match = AND)
builder.extraCondHint: e.g. composition <0>and</0> crystal system. Leave empty to match on a single value.
builder.extraCondIncomplete: Give each condition a unique name (distinct from the concept) and a field per dataset.
builder.step5Hint: You can keep multiple "perspectives (ways to connect)" distinct
builder.perspectivePlaceholder: e.g. Connect by composition (leave blank to overwrite the standard "composition" perspective)
builder.perspectiveNamed: Creates a new independent perspective (its own data, listed with your datasets).
builder.perspectiveDefault: When blank, creates/updates the standard "composition" perspective.
builder.buildHintInvalid: Enter the concept as an alphanumeric key (e.g. crystal_system).
builder.buildHintFewer: You can build once two or more datasets have a field picked (currently {{count}}).

result.head: Crosswalk built
result.skipped: Excluded: {{labels}} (out of scope, e.g. not published)
result.next: In "Crosswalk" you can check the participating datasets and tools (which datasets report this value?).

ops.discover: Looking for pairs that could connect
